# AgentXchain.dev

> Governed multi-agent software delivery. The protocol, runners, connectors, and workflow kit for lights-out software factories.

AgentXchain is an open-source protocol and CLI for coordinating multiple AI agents in structured, governed software delivery. It treats multi-agent coding as a governed system with explicit roles, turn structure, artifact contracts, challenge requirements, decision history, and human sovereignty.

## Core Concepts

- **Protocol**: Constitutional layer defining run state, role definitions, turn contracts, artifact contracts, validation rules, decision ledger, gates, and recovery rules.
- **Runners**: Execution engines that apply the protocol (local CLI runner, future hosted runners, CI/automation runners).
- **Connectors**: Bridges to agent runtimes — local AI coding agents, cloud AI agents, model APIs, IDE surfaces (VS Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenCode).
- **Workflow Kit**: Opinionated operating model for planning, specs, implementation, QA, release, escalation, recovery, and documentation.
- **Integrations**: Dashboards, notifications, ticketing, compliance hooks, release surfaces.

## Key Features

- Governed multi-agent coordination with structured turns and mandatory challenge
- Open-ended roles: any charter-driven role (architect, security_reviewer, tech_writer, etc.)
- Phase gates with artifact contracts and workflow-kit validation
- Multi-repo orchestration with coordinator pattern
- Continuous delivery intake lifecycle (record → triage → plan → execute → verify → resolve)
- Historical turn replay and audit (`verify turn` for staged, `replay turn` for accepted history)
- Full governed inspection family: `audit` (live governance report), `diff` (run comparison), `report`, `events` (lifecycle stream), `history` (accepted turns)
- Role/phase/gate introspection: `role list/show`, `turn show`, `phase list/show`, `gate list/show --evaluate`
- Project health: `doctor` (static health check with plugin/schedule/connector handoff), `connector check` (live probes)
- Human-sovereign approvals and escalation
- Provider-agnostic: works with any LLM (Anthropic, OpenAI, local models)
- Dashboard observation and governance reporting

## Documentation

- [Quickstart](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/quickstart): Install and run your first governed multi-agent session in under 5 minutes.
- [Getting Started Tutorial](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/getting-started): Step-by-step guide to setting up governed delivery.
- [Build Your First Governed Project in 5 Minutes](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/five-minute-tutorial): Fast manual-first path from install to your first accepted governed turn using the generic scaffold.
- [First Turn Guide](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/first-turn): What happens in a governed turn — artifacts, challenge, gates.
- [Tutorial: Install to Completion](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/tutorial): Complete zero-API-key walkthrough from scaffold to approve-completion — every command copy-pasteable.
- [CLI Reference](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/cli): Complete command reference for the agentxchain CLI.
- [Export Schema](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/export-schema): JSON schema for governed run exports.
- [Governance Report](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/governance-report): Understanding the auto-generated governance report.
- [Policies](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/policies): Declarative acceptance rules that differ from gates and hooks.
- [Approval Policy](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/approval-policy): Conditional auto-approval for phase transitions and run completion — the middle ground between manual gates and --auto-approve.
- [Gate Actions](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/gate-actions): Repo-local post-approval automation for human-approved gates, including dry-run preview, retry-safe execution, and decision-ledger evidence.
- [Timeouts](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/timeouts): Governed time budgets for turns, phases, and runs, including report/status visibility and recovery.
- [Templates](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/templates): Built-in and custom governed templates (generic, api-service, cli-tool, library, web-app, enterprise-app).
- [Choosing a Template](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/choosing-a-template): Decision guide for manual-first generic baseline vs project-type templates (api-service, cli-tool, library, web-app, enterprise-app).
- [Examples Hub](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples): Index of governed product examples, runner proofs, MCP examples, and remote execution examples.
- [Protocol v7 Reference](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/protocol): Full protocol specification.
- [Protocol Reference](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/protocol-reference): Protocol data structures and contracts.
- [Runner Interface](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/runner-interface): How to integrate with the runner execution engine.
- [Build Your Own Runner](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/build-your-own-runner): Guide to implementing a custom runner.
- [Protocol Implementor Guide](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/protocol-implementor-guide): For teams building protocol-compatible tools.
- [Remote Verification](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/remote-verification): Verifying governed runs across distributed systems.
- [Continuous Delivery Intake](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/continuous-delivery-intake): The intake lifecycle for continuous delivery.
- [Multi-Repo Orchestration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/multi-repo): Coordinating governed runs across multiple repositories.
- [Lights-Out Scheduling](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/lights-out-scheduling): Configure repo-local schedules, run the daemon, and let AgentXchain drive autonomous governed delivery on cadence.
- [Run Chaining](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/run-chaining): Auto-chain governed runs — when a run completes, automatically start a continuation with inherited context for lights-out operation.
- [Missions](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/missions): Repo-local long-horizon grouping above chained runs, including mission binding, derived status, and dashboard visibility.
- [Parallel Turns](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/parallel-turns): Run up to 4 concurrent governed agent turns per phase for faster multi-role delivery.
- [Delegation Chains](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/delegation-chains): Hierarchical task delegation — a role can delegate sub-tasks to other roles and review the results within a governed run.
- [Integration Guide](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integration-guide): Connect any agent to AgentXchain governance — HTTP services, MCP servers, or direct LLM APIs. Three proven patterns with real evidence.
- [Runtime Matrix](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/runtime-matrix): Canonical reference for all five runtimes, three authority levels, and the valid binding matrix.
- [Authority Model](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/authority-model): How `write_authority`, runtime type, and downstream CLI sandbox/approval mode interact.
- [Adapters](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/adapters): Provider adapters for Anthropic, OpenAI, and custom LLMs.
- [Build Your Own Connector](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/build-your-own-connector): Step-by-step tutorial to build a minimal HTTP agent connector, wire it into governance, and prove it works.
- [Integrations Hub](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations): Platform-specific setup guides for all supported IDEs, model runners, and API providers.
- [Claude Code Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/claude-code): Connect Claude Code via local_cli adapter.
- [OpenAI Codex CLI Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/openai-codex-cli): Connect Codex CLI via local_cli adapter.
- [VS Code Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/vscode): Official VS Code extension + local CLI agents.
- [Ollama Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/ollama): Run local models via Ollama's OpenAI-compatible API.
- [Anthropic Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/anthropic): Claude Opus 4.6, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5 via api_proxy.
- [OpenAI Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/openai): GPT-5.4, GPT-5.3-Codex, GPT-OSS via api_proxy.
- [Google Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/google): Gemini 3.1 Pro, Flash, Gemma 4 via api_proxy.
- [MCP Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/mcp): Any MCP-compatible agent via stdio or Streamable HTTP.
- [OpenClaw Integration](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/integrations/openclaw): Connect OpenClaw (100K+ stars) via local_cli adapter; AgentXchain plugin for OpenClaw also available.
- [Plugins](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/plugins): Plugin system for extending governed runs.
- [Slack Notify Plugin](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/plugins/slack-notify): Built-in Slack incoming-webhook plugin for acceptance, gate, and escalation notifications.
- [JSON Report Plugin](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/plugins/json-report): Built-in repo-local report writer for machine-readable lifecycle artifacts.
- [GitHub Issues Plugin](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/plugins/github-issues): Built-in GitHub issue mirror with one comment per run and managed phase/blocked labels.
- [Workflow Kit](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/workflow-kit): AgentXchain's opinionated operating model — planning, specs, QA, release, and recovery as a separable protocol layer.
- [Notifications](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/notifications): Webhook and notification hooks.
- [Recovery](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/recovery): Recovery procedures for failed or blocked runs.
- [Multi-Session Continuity](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/multi-session): How governed runs survive process boundaries — resume, recover, and complete across separate sessions.

## Comparisons

- [AgentXchain vs CrewAI](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/compare-crewai): How AgentXchain's governed protocol compares to CrewAI's crew-based task orchestration.
- [AgentXchain vs AutoGen](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/compare-autogen): How AgentXchain's structured governance compares to AutoGen's conversational multi-agent approach.
- [AgentXchain vs LangGraph](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/compare-langgraph): How AgentXchain's protocol-first design compares to LangGraph's graph-based agent orchestration.

## Example Library

### Products

- [Governed Todo App](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/governed-todo-app)
- [Habit Board](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/habit-board)
- [Trail Meals Mobile](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/trail-meals-mobile)
- [Async Standup Bot](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/async-standup-bot)
- [Decision Log Linter](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/decision-log-linter)
- [Schema Guard](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/schema-guard)

### Proofs

- [CI Runner Proof](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/ci-runner-proof)
- [External Runner Starter](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/external-runner-starter)
- [Live Governed Proof](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/live-governed-proof)
- [Checkpoint Handoff Proof](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/checkpoint-handoff-proof)
- [Live Continuous 3-Run Proof](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/live-continuous-3run-proof)
- [MCP Echo Agent](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/mcp-echo-agent)
- [MCP HTTP Echo Agent](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/mcp-http-echo-agent)
- [MCP Anthropic Agent](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/mcp-anthropic-agent)
- [Remote Agent Bridge](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/remote-agent-bridge)
- [Remote Conformance Server](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/examples/remote-conformance-server)

## Comparison Pages

- [vs Devin](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-devin): AgentXchain vs Devin (autonomous AI coding agent).
- [vs MetaGPT](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-metagpt): AgentXchain vs MetaGPT (SOP-driven multi-agent framework).
- [vs Codegen](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-codegen): AgentXchain vs Codegen (enterprise code-agent platform).
- [vs OpenHands](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-openhands): AgentXchain vs OpenHands (open-source agent platform).
- [vs Warp.dev](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-warp): AgentXchain vs Warp (AI-native terminal).
- [vs CrewAI](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-crewai): AgentXchain vs CrewAI (multi-agent orchestration).
- [vs LangGraph](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-langgraph): AgentXchain vs LangGraph (stateful agent graphs).
- [vs OpenAI Agents SDK](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-openai-agents-sdk): AgentXchain vs OpenAI Agents SDK.
- [vs AutoGen](https://agentxchain.dev/compare/vs-autogen): AgentXchain vs AutoGen (conversational multi-agent).

## Case Study

- [How AgentXchain Built AgentXchain](https://agentxchain.dev/docs/case-study-self-build): Evidence-backed case study of two AI agents (Claude Opus 4.6 + GPT 5.4) building AgentXchain itself — 1,140+ commits, 130+ decisions, 4,350+ tests, 190+ governed turns.

## Other Pages

- [Why AgentXchain](https://agentxchain.dev/why): The problem AgentXchain solves and why governance matters.
- [Launch Announcement](https://agentxchain.dev/launch): Product launch details and positioning.
- [Homepage](https://agentxchain.dev/): Landing page with architecture overview and getting started.

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g agentxchain
# or
brew tap shivamtiwari93/tap && brew install agentxchain
```

## Quick Example

```bash
agentxchain init --governed --template generic
agentxchain run --provider anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-6
agentxchain report
```

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/shivamtiwari93/agentXchain.dev
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/agentxchain
- Cloud Platform: https://agentxchain.ai
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/agentxchain-dev/
- Reddit: https://www.reddit.com/r/agentXchain_dev/
